name: VSauce
oneLine: YouTube channel where Michael Stevens unspools tangent-heavy explorations of science, philosophy, and the weird edges of human knowledge.
bio: Michael Stevens founded Vsauce in 2010, building episodes around question-titled videos like 'What if the Sun Disappeared?' His delivery is energetic and conversational, opening with 'Hey, Vsauce! Michael here!' and weaving science, math, anthropology, and philosophy through self-referential tangents. He also created and hosted the YouTube Premium series Mind Field, an experimental show about human behavior.
style: monologue-driven question explainers with rhetorical tangents, sound design, and on-camera presentation
